name: Barbecued Beef Roll
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the steak to 1/2 to 3/4-inch thick and weigh approximately 2 to 2 1/2 lbs.
2. Combine catsup, chili sauce, brown sugar, wine vinegar, steak sauce, cumin, and chili powder in a small saucepan; cook slowly for 20 minutes.
3. Remove the bone from the round steak and sprinkle with tenderizer, salt, and pepper. Pound with a mallet.
4. Brush the top of the steak with half a cup of barbecue sauce.
5. Sprinkle with shredded Cheddar cheese, corn, green pepper, and sliced ripe olives.
6. Begin with the long side, roll the steak tightly and tie with string.
7. Dredge the steak roll in flour and brown lightly in cooking oil in an electric frying pan.
8. Pour remaining barbecue sauce and water over the steak.
9. Cover tightly and cook slowly for 1 1/2 hours or until the meat is tender.
10. During the last 5 minutes of cooking time, remove strings and sprinkle the top of the meat with shredded Cheddar cheese and sliced ripe olives.
11. Place the steak roll on a warm serving platter.
12. Garnish with tomato roses.
13. Place green pepper slices around the roses for a leaf effect.
14. Serve remaining barbecue sauce with the beef roll.
NOTE:
Tomato Roses: Using 3 tomatoes, pare each with a very sharp knife, starting at the rounded top and continuing in a circular manner around the tomato to obtain a strip of peel approximately 10 inches long and 1 inch wide (pare off only the skin). Wrap one end of one strip around the tip of your index finger. Continue wrapping, forming the petals of a rose. Gently lift the tomato rose from the finger tip to the serving platter. Repeat for other roses.
